Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs JEFFER MANGELS BUTLER & MITCHELL LLP and ROBERT BRAUN's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Off calendar. On January 7, 2019 this case was transferred to Los Angeles Superior Court.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Continued To Mar-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Joseph Salama of the law offices of Joseph Salama (415-948-9030) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kristin Iversen of the law offices of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415-788-1900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs STEVEN FRIEDMAN and MICHAEL FRIEDMAN's LAW OFFICES OF STEVEN FRIEDMAN MOTION TO TRANSFER venue to Los Angeles County is GRANTED. This case is transferred to central district of Los Angeles County Superior Court and all transfer costs must be paid by plaintiff Nicole Tate-Naghi. Venue is presumptively proper in the county where any of the defendants resides. (CCP 395(a).) The first amended complaint alleges that all of the defendants reside in Los Angeles County. Ms. Tate-Naghi has alleged transitory causes of action for professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty that do not involve the internal affairs of the trust. The conversion cause of action merely asserts that these alleged breaches of professional and fiduciary duties harmed the trust, which does not implicate the internal affairs of the trust. Probate Code sections 17000 and 17005 are inapplicable and Ms. Tate-Naghi's arguments that this case should remain with the two trust cases in San Francisco lack merit. Transferring this case to Los Angeles is not inconsistent with the decision of the Los Angeles Superior Court transferring the probate case to San Francisco because this case is not a probate division case and does not implicate the internal affairs of the trust. Furthermore, Haines v. Lamb (1962) 206 Cal.App.2d 322 and CCP 395.1 are inapplicable because this case is not a lawsuit against an executor, administrator, guardian, conservator, or trustee. Defendants' request for fees and expenses per CCP 396b(b) is denied in the exercise of the court's discretion because Ms. Tate-Naghi had a good faith, albeit mistaken, basis for filing this case in San Francisco and her untimely filed opposition papers did not unfairly prejudice the defendants. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.com (L&M302)
